sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I clicked the top diamond block (50,30) and it turned GREEN! Now the grid has:
- All 5 corner blocks: GREEN
- Top diamond block: GREEN (was WHITE)
- Other 3 diamond blocks: still WHITE
So clicking white diamond blocks turns them GREEN too! The pattern is:
- Click any white block (diamond or corner) to turn it GREEN
- The goal might be to turn ALL blocks GREEN
Let me click the remaining 3 white diamond blocks to see if that completes the level.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Clicked (50,30) - top diamond turned GREEN!
# Now: corners all GREEN, top diamond GREEN, 3 diamonds still WHITE
# Hypothesis: need ALL blocks GREEN to complete level
# Click remaining 3 white diamond blocks

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 25}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 35}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 30}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>

Testing if all green completes level."}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Post-game-over: 8 blocks green (5 corners + top diamond), 3 diamond blocks still white", "goal_model": "Turn all 9 blocks green to complete level", "recent_findings": ["Clicking white diamond blocks turns them green"], "open_questions": ["Does all-green grid complete the level?"], "current_plan": "Click remaining 3 white diamond blocks", "strategy_log": ["After corners green, clicking diamond blocks also turns them green"], "failed_probes": []}}
